docs.title: Training backends
docs.text: class description xtrain.Core No model JIT compiling, i.e., for debugging xtrain.JIT JIT compile the model. Default strategy xtrain.VMapped Transform the model with vmap. This allows defining a model on unbatched data but train with batched data. xtrain.Distributed Transform the model with pmap. This allows training the model on multiple devices.

docs.title: xtrain.Trainer
docs.text:
A general purpose FLAX model trainer. Help avoiding most of the biolerplate code when trainning with FLAX.

Attributes:

Name Type Description model

A Flax module

losses

A list of loss function (or other callabels).

optimizer Optimizer

An optax optimizer

seed

RNG seed

params Params

Current model parameters. This is a frozen dict. This is read/write.

initialized

Whether the model has been initialized with an optimizer and initial weights.

Example:

```
trainer = xtrain.Trainer(my_module, my_loss_func)

trainer.initialize(my_dataset, tx=my_optimzier)

train_it = trainer.train(my_dataset)

for k in range(train_steps):
    loss_logs = next(train_it)
    if k % 1000 == 0:
        print(loss_logs)
        trainer.reset()
```

docs.title: __call__(inputs, *, strategy=None, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Calling the underlying model

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default inputs Any

A tuple or a dict as the inputs for the model.

required strategy Optional[type]

Optionally override the default strategy.

None **kwargs

Additional keyword args passed on to the model

{}

Returns:

Type Description Any

Model outputs.

docs.title: __init__(model, losses=None, optimizer=None, seed=42, strategy=strategy.JIT)
docs.text:
Constructor

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default model Module

A Flax module. It can be a bound module with parameters.

required losses Optional[LOSSES]

A loss function (callabels) or a list of loss functions, or None. These should have the call signiture of: loss = loss_fn(inputs, labels, preds) The "preds" is the model output. The "inputs" and "labels" are from the train dataset. The model trains on the sum of all losses.

None optimizer Optional[Optimizer]

An optax optimzier

None seed Union[int, Array]

RNG seed.

42 strategy type

Training backend. See Traing backends.

JIT

docs.title: initialize(data, tx=None)
docs.text:
Initialize the model weights and optimizer states.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default data Iterable

An iterator or iterable to produce training dataset. It is not used if model is bound with weights already. see train()

required tx Optional[Optimizer]

Optional optax optimzier for when the object was constructed without an optimizer

None

docs.title: pickle_self(path)
docs.text:
Make a pickle save of the trainer. This saves the model as well as the training states.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default path PathLike

The file path.

required

docs.title: reset(loss_weights=None, losses=None)
docs.text:
Reset internal loss value tracking

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default loss_weights Optional[Sequence[float]]

Optional weights of individual loss functions. If not None, the total loss is weighted sum.

None losses Optional[LOSSES]

Optionally override the class losses defined as the object initialization.

None

docs.title: restore_from_pickle(path) staticmethod
docs.text:
Restore from the a pickled saved.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default path PathLike

The pickle file path.

required

Returns:

Type Description

A new trainer object.

docs.title: save_model(path, sub_module=None)
docs.text:
Save the model in a pickled file. The pickle is a tuple of (module, weights).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default path PathLike

The file path.

required sub_module Optional[str]

Optionally only save a sub_module of the model by specifying the name

None

docs.title: test(dataset, metrics, strategy=None, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Create test/validation iterator.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default dataset Iterable

An iterator or iterable to supply the testing data. The iterator should yield a tupple of (inputs, labels).

required metrics METRICS

A list of Metric objects. They should have two functions: m.update(preds, **kwargs): preds is the model output. the remaining kwargs are content of labels. m.compute(): which should return the accumulated metric value.

required strategy Optional[type]

Optionally override the default strategy.

None

Returns:

Type Description Iterator

An iterator. Stepping through it will drive the updating of each metric obj. The iterator itself return the list of metrics.

docs.title: test_and_compute(*args, **kwargs)
docs.text:
A convient function to compute all metrics. See test() fucntion

Returns:

Type Description dict

A metric dict. Keys are metric names.

docs.title: train(dataset, strategy=None, rng_cols=None, **kwargs)
docs.text:
Create the training iterator

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default dataset Iterable

An iterator or iterable to supply the training data. The dataset should produce (inputs, labels, sample_weight), however both the labels and the sample_weight are optional. The inputs is either a tuple or a dict. If the inputs is a dict, the keys are interpreted as the names for keyword args of the model's call function.

required strategy Optional[type]

Optionally override the default strategy.

None rng_cols Sequence[str]

Names of any RNG used by the model. Should be a list of strings.

None **kwargs

Additional keyward args passed to the model. E.g. "training=True"

{}

Returns:

Type Description Iterator

A iterator. Stepping through the iterator will train the model. The iterator itself returns a loss log dict, which are mean loss values for each loss function.

docs.title: xtrain.TFDatasetAdapter
docs.text:
Convert tf.data.Dataset into a python iterable suitable for xtrain.Trainer

my_dataset = TFDatasetAdapter(my_tf_dataset)

docs.title: xtrain.TorchDataLoaderAdapter
docs.text:
Convert torch dataloader into a python iterable suitable for xtrain.Trainer

my_dataset = TorchDataLoaderAdapter(my_torch_dataloader)

docs.title: xtrain.loss_func_on(func, filters)
docs.text:
A decorator for loss functions to select specific subcompoenent

Some models output multiple outputs and a loss needed to calculated on each subcomponent. The decorator makes it easy to generate new loss functions that operate on a subcompoenent of the output. e.g.

# compute loss only on the first element of the model output
new_loss_func = loss_func_on(orig_loss_func, 0)

docs.title: xtrain.partial_loss_func(func, *args, **kwargs)
docs.text:
A "partial"-like decorator for loss functions

It differs from functools.partial in that in keeps the name properties so that the Trainer can report loss logs with a human readable name.
